title: FireAngel Carbon Monoxide Alarm and Detector - FA6813 10-Year Sensor Life CO Alarm for Home with AA Batteries - Travel, Portable Carbon Monoxide Detector - CO Detector Monitor with Test Button, Single
features: CARBON MONOXIDE ALARMS FOR HOME USE: The advanced electrochemical sensor detects dangerous levels of carbon monoxide (CO) in your home, alerting you with 4 loud chirps (85 dB(A) at 3 metres) and a flashing red ‘CO’ LED light. NEVER IGNORE THIS ALARM.

TEST BUTTON: Press the large test button to ensure your CO detector is working properly. A loud, high-pitched sound and the red LED flashing four times will verify that the alarm is functioning correctly.

BATTERIES INCLUDED: Each CO monitor comes with 2 replaceable AA batteries. A flashing amber '!' and an audible chirp will alert you when the batteries need replacing. Test your FireAngel carbon monoxide alarm regularly to ensure it is operational.

SLEEP EASY FUNCTION: Press the large test button to silence the low battery alert. This activates the Sleep Easy mode on the carbon monoxide monitor, muting the low battery chirp for 8 hours whilst the amber light flashes. Replace the batteries ASAP.

TRAVEL CARBON MONOXIDE DETECTOR: Each portable device comes with an integrated mounting bracket and screws, allowing for easy wall mounting. Measuring 120 x 73 x 35.5 mm, it’s compact enough to travel with, providing on the go holiday protection.

SAFETY ASSURED: The FA6813 detector holds a CE & UKCA mark and has been designed to conform to the standards BS EN 50291-1: 2008 and EN 50291-2: 2019. Suitable for homes in England and Wales, but not for Scottish homes under the new regulations.

FIREANGEL HOME PROTECTION: In addition to our carbon monoxide detectors, we offer a wide range of other safety products designed to protect you and your family. Choose from smart and wireless devices, as well as standard fire, smoke, and heat alarms.
description: From the manufacturer Breathe Easy At FireAngel, we're dedicated to protecting and improving our customers’ lives by making innovative, cutting-edge fire safety technology that’s simple and accessible. The FA6813’s electrochemical sensing technology enables a fast reaction to rising levels of carbon monoxide (CO), aka ‘The Silent Killer’, helping to keep you and your family safe. LOUD ALARM: If dangerous levels of CO are detected in your home, the carbon monoxide detector will alert you with 4 loud chirps—85 dB(A) at 3 m (min)—and a flashing red ‘CO’ LED light. TEST BUTTON: Press the large test button to ensure your CO detector is working properly. A loud, high-pitched sound and the red LED flashing four times will verify that the alarm is functioning correctly. Test your device regularly (we recommend once a week). LOW BATTERY ALERT: A flashing amber '!' and an audible chirp will alert you when the batteries need replacing. SLEEP EASY FUNCTION: If the low battery alert sounds overnight and you are unable to immediately replace the batteries, press the large test button to silence the low battery alert. This activates the Sleep Easy mode on the carbon monoxide monitor, muting the low battery chirp for 8 hours while the amber light flashes. Replace the batteries ASAP. CRESCENDO TEST: This feature helps to protect your hearing by allowing you to choose the volume level of the test alarm. ALARM MEMORY: With the alarm memory function, a red LED will flash to let you know with a visual indication that there has been a previous CO alarm activation within the last 30 days. Ventilate and call a technician. REPLACEABLE BATTERIES: Each device is fitted with 1 year replaceable batteries. UK SAFETY STANDARDS: The FA6813 detector holds a CE & UKCA mark and has been designed to conform to the standards BS EN 50291-1: 2008 and EN 50291-2: 2019. FireAngel: Smarter solutions for safer homes. CE & UKCA Mark The FireAngel FA6813 carbon monoxide alarm meets the safety standards BS EN 50291-1: 2008 and EN 50291-2: 2019. Holds a CE & UKCA mark. BSI Kitemark certified. Suitable for homes in England and Wales, but not for Scottish homes under the new regulations. 10-Year Promise Each CO alarm has a 10-year sensor life, ensuring peace of mind and protection for your family. Additional features include the Crescendo Test to protect your hearing. Hold down the test button, and the alarm will increase in volume until you find the test alert sound level you are comfortable with. At Home and Abroad The FA6813 portable free-standing carbon monoxide detector is equipped with an integrated mounting bracket and 2 screws for easy wall mounting. It’s also compact enough to travel with. Measures 120 x 73 x 35.5 mm for portability. A holiday essential to keep you and your family protected when travelling abroad. Sensor Type Electrochemical Battery Life/Type Minimum 1 year / 2 x 1.5V DC AA Alkaline batteries Sensor Life 10 Years Dimensions 120mm (width) x 73mm (height) x 35.5mm (depth) Supply Voltage 3V DC Alarm Sound Level 85dB(A) at 3m (minimum) Installation Wall / Free-standing (portable – suitable for home and/or travel use) For Use In Domestic premises Operating Temperature -10 °C to +40 °C Operating Humidity 25 - 95% RH (non condensing) Storage Temperature -20 °C to +50 °C Storage Humidity 30 - 90% RH (non condensing) Weight 146g What should I do if the carbon monoxide (CO) alarm sounds? In the event of the CO alarm sounding, follow these 3 steps: VENTILATE : Keep calm; open doors and windows to ventilate your home. Stop using fuel-burning appliances and turn them off. EVACUATE : Evacuate the property immediately. RING FOR HELP: If in the UK, call The National Gas Emergency Service on 0800 111 999. If outside the UK, call your supplier's emergency number. Do not use appliances until they have been checked by an expert. Get medical help for anyone suffering the effects of CO poisoning. What are the symptoms of CO poisoning? Symptoms of CO poisoning include headache, dizziness, weakness, nausea and vomiting, rapid heartbeat, shortness of breath, seizures, chest pain, disorientation, and loss of consciousness. Who should I call in the event of a CO leak? If in the UK, call The National Gas Emergency Service on 0800 111 999. If outside the UK, call your supplier's emergency number. What are the sources of CO? The common sources of Carbon Monoxide (CO) are cookers, fires, boilers, oil and gas boilers, portable generators, oil or solid fuel cookers, gas or paraffin heaters, barbecues (BBQs), clogged chimneys, wood or gas fireplaces, cigarette smoke or any fossil fuel‑burning appliances. About Us We pride ourselves on product reliability and quality. Since 1998, we’ve deployed over 80 million safety products, protecting over 25 million homes nationwide. Sustainability We’re committed to reducing our impact on the planet; that’s why we've reduced the plastic in our packaging and have unique products that use less energy. The FireAngel range In addition to our carbon monoxide detectors for the home, we offer a wide range of other safety products designed to protect you and your family. Choose from smart and wireless devices, as well as standard fire, smoke, and heat alarms.
